title: Control flow statements should not be used inside taskgroup or nursery blocks
description: Avoid using control flow statements (if, while, for, etc.) inside taskgroup or nursery blocks. These blocks are designed for parallel execution of coroutines and the use of control flow statements can lead to unexpected behavior.
message: Control flow statement found inside taskgroup or nursery block
recommendation: Refactor the code to use parallelism without control flow statements

noncompliant.code:
async def non_compliant():
    taskgroup = asyncio.create_task_group()
    if condition:
        taskgroup.start_soon(some_func)
    await taskgroup.join()
noncompliant.description: Control flow statement (if) used inside taskgroup

compliant.code:
async def compliant():
    taskgroup = asyncio.create_task_group()
    task1 = taskgroup.start_soon(some_func1)
    task2 = taskgroup.start_soon(some_func2)
    await taskgroup.join()
compliant.description: No control flow statements used inside taskgroup

why_is_this_an_issue: Control flow statements can lead to unexpected behavior when used inside taskgroup or nursery blocks, as they can cause the execution order to deviate from the intended parallel execution.
how_to_fix: Refactor the code to use parallelism without control flow statements

metadata.title: Control flow statements should not be used inside taskgroup or nursery blocks
